title: aspidium viscidulum
intro: Aspidium viscidulum, also known as the sticky fern or black spleenwort, is a type of plant species belonging to the Aspidium genus in the family Polypodiaceae, characterized by its dark green fronds with a sticky rachis. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Aspidium viscidulum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and swelling, and even act as a natural remedy for digestive issues and skin problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tea, tincture, or powder, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or compress to treat various skin conditions.
